notes: Chobham composite — original UK special armor developed at Chobham Common (FERMAN/MACLEAN projects, 1960s). Ceramic tiles in resin matrix between steel plates. RHAe ~2.0-2.4 per mm vs KE. Used in Challenger 1, early M1 Abrams prototypes. Superior to Burlington in later variants.

source.reference: Classified UK MoD armor program, open-source analysis (FERMAN/MACLEAN)
source.methodology: RHAe estimated from declassified references and indirect data: Chobham was reported as '2-3x RHA per weight'. Value 2.2 represents approximate thickness-efficiency midpoint. Precise layup remains classified.
